advantages: Immutable history → reproducibility, audit trail and easy rollback.
Branches are O(1) cheap, enabling per-feature/per-experiment isolation.
Content-addressed hashes (SHA-1) detect tampering and accidental corruption.
Distributed model → no central single point of failure for the history itself.
disadvantages: Mental model is unusual (commits, branches, HEAD, refs, index) and trips up newcomers.
Large binary files bloat history forever — needs LFS or careful gitignore.
Force-pushes and rewrites can erase shared history if done carelessly.
Power-tools (rebase, reflog, cherry-pick) are sharp; misuse loses work.
